sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good progress! I clicked on 6 green blocks (the ones under the yellow dots) and the green count went from 14 to 8. The board didn't transform yet, and the game didn't end. This confirms that clicking on green blocks under the yellow dots is the correct approach.
Now I have 8 green blocks remaining (the ones NOT under the yellow dots, i.e., columns 31-35, 37-41, 43-47, 49-53). I need to continue removing them.
Looking at the current image:
- Green blocks are now only in the right 4 columns (cols 31-35, 37-41, 43-47, 49-53)
- Character is at the right side (cols 44-45 based on previous position + 5 RIGHT moves)
- The first 3 columns of green blocks (under yellow dots) are gone
Let me continue clicking on the remaining 8 green blocks. I should click on them in order from left to right.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Good! Clicked on 6 green blocks under yellow dots, green count 14->8
# Remaining green blocks are in cols 31-35, 37-41, 43-47, 49-53 (4 columns)
# Need to remove remaining 8 green blocks

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Remaining green blocks:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Click on remaining green blocks
# Top row: cols 31-35, 37-41, 43-47, 49-53 -> centers at 33, 39, 45, 51
# Bottom row: same cols
clicks = [
    (27, 33), (27, 39), (27, 45), (27, 51),  # top row
    (33, 33), (33, 39), (33, 45), (33, 51)   # bottom row
]

for r, c in clicks:
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c}])
    green_count = sum(1 for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N')
    print(f"Click ({r},{c}): green={green_count}, gameplay_changed={last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}")
    if last_action_result.get('game_over') or last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
        break

print(f"\nFinal: level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
